example: "Llevo el teléfono por si acaso." (I am carrying the phone just in case.)
"Este viaje es caro, pero vale la pena." (This trip is expensive, but it is worth it.)
"Dije que iríamos al parque y dicho y hecho, aquí estamos." (I said we would go to the park and said and done, here we are.)
"A estas alturas, ya sé que él no viene" (By now, I already know that he is not coming)
"Le dije que necesitaba ayuda y, dicho y hecho, vino a mi casa" (I told him I needed help and, no sooner said than done, he came to my house)

phrase: A falta de pan, buenas son tortas
meaning: Half a loaf is better than none
example: "No hay vino tinto, pero aquí tienes vino blanco. A falta de pan, buenas son tortas" (There is no red wine, but here you have white wine. Half a loaf is better than none)
"Me di cuenta de que olvidé la maleta en el taxi" (I realized that I forgot the suitcase in the taxi)

meaning: The apple doesn't fall far from the tree
example: "Tu hijo es muy inteligente, como tú. De tal palo, tal astilla." (Your son is very intelligent, like you. The apple doesn't fall far from the tree.)

meaning: In the absence of / Due to a lack of
example: "A falta de café, estoy bebiendo té" (In the absence of coffee, I am drinking tea)

meaning: to pull someone's leg / to tease
example: "¿Me estás tomando el pelo o es verdad?" (Are you pulling my leg or is it true?)
"Si queremos aprobar el examen de mañana, tenemos que ponernos las pilas." (If we want to pass tomorrow's exam, we have to get our act together.)
"De vez en cuando nado en el mar" (From time to time I swim in the sea)

phrase: Tener los pies en la tierra
meaning: To have one's feet on the ground
example: "Mi hermano siempre tiene los pies en la tierra" (My brother always has his feet on the ground)
"Quiero reunirme con mi hermano para ponernos al día." (I want to meet up with my brother to catch up.)
"A estas alturas, ya sabemos toda la verdad." (At this point, we already know the whole truth.)
"Echo de menos a mi familia cuando viajo." (I miss my family when I travel.)
"Voy a cruzar los dedos para que apruebes el examen." (I am going to cross my fingers so that you pass the exam.)
"El jefe decidió hacer la vista gorda hoy." (The boss decided to turn a blind eye today.)

meaning: to make a mistake / mess up
example: "Metí la pata cuando le pregunté sobre su exnovio." (I messed up when I asked her about her ex-boyfriend.)
